boundaries: Research-only static analysis. Not investment advice. This tool does not fetch live data, connect to brokers, place orders, recommend buys, sells, holds, target allocations, or trades, predict returns, or produce personalized financial advice.

questions.answer: Run command-matrix, fixture-doctor, build-packet, and public-readiness from the repository root.
questions.question: What should a first-time GitHub visitor run first?

questions.answer: No. The package reads static CSV inputs and bundled examples only.
questions.question: Does the project use live macro, market, or broker data?

questions.answer: Regenerate demo outputs, run release-manifest, then run diff-check against the saved manifest.
questions.question: How can an evaluator verify artifact drift?

questions.answer: Use citation-map for claim-to-artifact references and artifact-index for hashes and producer commands.
questions.question: Where are public claims linked to evidence?

questions.answer: Live feeds, workflow automation, broker connections, trade actions, return predictions, and personalized financial advice.
questions.question: What is intentionally unsupported?

questions.answer: Use boundary-attestation, provenance-ledger, reproducibility-recipe, and release-notes-draft for v1.3.0 governance evidence.
questions.question: Where is the governance and attestation layer?

title: Release FAQ
